title: Comforting Creamy Malaysian Sirloin Steak Noodles
description: A fermented and fluffy Malaysian dish featuring sirloin steak and beurre-monte, perfect for a sick day, and friendly to those who dislike onion and garlic.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the beurre-monte over medium-high heat and cook the sirloin steak strips until browned and cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees.
In a small bowl, whisk together the fish sauce, soy sauce, and lime juice.
Add the fermented shrimp paste to the bowl and whisk until smooth.
Pour the coconut milk into the bowl and whisk until well combined.
Add the cooked noodles to the skillet with the steak and pour the sauce over the top.
Stir everything together and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
